summary: Fixed REQUEST_CHANGES_R2 blockers by requiring objective-complete handoff commands to name an explicit march matching the surrendered siege, and by giving the orderly-withdrawal grain return ledger a stable source and reservation-field tie-breaker.

tests_run.result: 1 file and 11 tests passed, including objective-complete marchId null rejection and deterministic shared-reservation return-ledger allocation.
Node and worker-compatible hashes matched; command/query hash d275faa4; save/load hash 28074edd.
Architecture boundaries passed; determinism passed; 32 root Vitest files and 223 tests passed; web 1 file and 3 tests passed.

tests_run.command: node -e JSON parse/shape check for M4-WITHDRAWAL-POSTWAR-HANDOFF-001 and REVIEW-R3 handoff JSON
tests_run.result: Task JSON and REVIEW-R3 handoff JSON parsed successfully with expected REVIEW status, systems_architect route, required keys, and tests_run shape.

decisions: Objective-complete withdrawal now rejects payload.marchId null before creating any WarOutcome or PostwarCandidate.
Objective-complete still requires completed campaign state, surrendered siege result, matching campaign target, and siege.marchId equal to the resolved march.marchId.

Return-ledger ordering now breaks reservationId ties with source kind, source districtId, source populationGroupId, reservation quantity fields, loss reason, expected supply fields, and status fields.
No protocol, save-format, UI, diplomacy, treaty, rebellion, naval, manual battle, M5 content, telemetry, server, multiplayer, arbitrary-code mod, or historical content scope was added.
risks: R3 remains: M4 authoritative command validation and deterministic accounting behavior changed and require systems_architect review.
The shared-reservation deterministic allocation regression uses allowed multi-source rows with the same reservationId; reviewers should confirm this matches the intended source-ledger semantics.
requested_action: Review the R3 fixes for objective-complete march-reference gating and deterministic source-ledger ordering; ACCEPT or REQUEST_CHANGES.
